channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The hidden vulnerability: Why legacy government web forms demand urgent attention
item.description: The cost of action is significant, but the cost of inaction — breached data, compromised systems, regulatory penalties and lost public trust — is far higher. The post The hidden vulnerability: Why legacy government web forms demand urgent attention first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla sales outlook darkens despite Musk’s self-driving euphoria
item.description: Tesla Inc. ended last year on a roll, with investors increasingly buying into Elon Musk's ebullience about autonomous vehicles. Winning over actual car buyers was another story.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: AI’s most important benchmark in 2026? Trust
item.description: In 2026 (and beyond) the best benchmark for large language models won’t be MMLU or AgentBench or GAIA. It will be trust—something AI will have to rebuild before it can be broadly useful and valuable to both consumers and businesses. Researchers identify several different kinds of AI trust.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Grok makes sexual images of kids as users test AI guardrails
item.description: Elon Musk's artificial intelligence chatbot Grok created sexualized images of minors on the social media platform X in response to user prompts in recent days, drawing criticism of a tool that positions itself as less restrained than its competition.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: One UI 8.5 leaks show off Samsung's new Privacy Display feature for phones, and Bixby powered by Perplexity AI
item.description: A major software update for Samsung phones is on the way, and we just got a preview of two of its new features.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: AI disclosure in healthcare: What patients must know
item.description: States require AI disclosure in healthcare as California, Colorado and Utah implement laws mandating transparency when artificial intelligence influences patient care decisions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Space X plans to lower Starlink satellites’ orbital altitude
item.description: Space X plans to lower the orbit of all Starlink satellites in 2026, Reuters reports. According to the private space company’s chief technology officer Michael Nicolls, the orbit of the satellites will be lowered from about 550 to 480 kilometers. The aim is to increase safety in space.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: America can’t afford to hollow out its cyber defenses
item.description: Jaya Baloo, the co-founder and COO of AISLE, explains why cuts to CISA will harm the nation’s ability to protect against AI cyber threats. The post America can’t afford to hollow out its cyber defenses first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to unlock the power of ChatGPT
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. I’ve recently written about free, private AI tools and the best AI mobile apps.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: 47 awesome Android app discoveries from 2025
item.description: It’s the calm before the storm. Today, on New Year’s Day, we have a brief moment to pause and prepare — and set ourselves up for success. From a tech perspective, that means taking the time to clean up and optimize your smartphone setup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China requires chipmakers to use at least 50% domestic equipment
item.description: According to Reuters, China is now requiring its chipmakers to use at least 50% domestically made equipment when expanding their production capacity. The rule is not publicly documented, but chipmakers must show that at least half of their equipment is Chinese to get government approval.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What is cloud computing? From infrastructure to autonomous, agentic-driven ecosystems
item.description: Cloud computing continues to be the platform of choice for large applications and a driver of innovation in enterprise technology. Gartner forecasts public cloud spending alone to the public cloud services market alone will reach $1.42 trillion in current U.S.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The top 10 AI stories of 2025
item.description: It’s safe to say that AI was the story of 2025: It topped CIOs’ tech agendas; vendors rolled out a constant stream of new AI products and features; and IT professionals and other knowledge workers got down to the business of integrating it into their workflows.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to manually update Microsoft Defender
item.description: Microsoft Defender is the built-in anti-malware package that’s included with modern Windows operating systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI agents 2025 recap: What happened and what to expect next year
item.description: In artificial intelligence, 2025 marked a decisive shift. Systems once confined to research labs and prototypes began to appear as everyday tools. At the center of this transition was the rise of AI agents—AI systems that can use other software tools and act on their own.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia licenses Groq’s inferencing chip tech and hires its leaders
item.description: Nvidia has licensed intellectual property from inferencing chip designer Groq, and hired away some of its senior executives, but stopped short of an outright acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What's inside Mexico's Popocatépetl? Scientists obtain first 3D images of the whole volcano
item.description: Scientists from Mexico’s National Autonomous University have created the first three-dimensional image of the Popocatépetl volcano's interior.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Understanding AI-native cloud: from microservices to model-serving
item.description: Cloud computing has fundamentally transformed the way enterprises operate. Initially built for more basic, everyday computing tasks, its capabilities have expanded exponentially with the advent of new technologies (such as machine learning and analytics).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: More than $32 billion in U.S. clean energy projects were canceled in 2025
item.description: Two years ago, a $575 million battery factory planned in St. Louis, Missouri, was set to be the first large-scale lithium iron phosphate (LFP) facility in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI looks for a new head of AI preparedness
item.description: OpenAI has announced that it is looking for a new Head of Preparedness, who will be responsible for anticipating and mitigating the risks and damages that its AI models can cause. As compensation, OpenAI is offering around $550,000 in annual salary, plus stock.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Using DISM to install Windows updates
item.description: Windows’ built-in Deployment Image Servicing and Management (DISM) command, a.k.a. dism.exe, is something of a Swiss Army knife when it comes to working on Windows OS images.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
